signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + COT vs OIS Divergence
signals.text: NZD is the weakest G10 currency this week, averaging -1.493% vs peers, while USD is the strongest at +0.903%, creating a 2.396% leader-to-laggard spread. NZD/USD COT LF net short −22,454 (long%=18.4%) represents an extreme speculative short position, diverging from the RBNZ's stated hike bias (next meeting 8 Jul). This crowded short faces significant unwind risk if the RBNZ signals or delivers a hike, especially given the current USD-favored carry of +150bp (SOFR 3.61% vs OCR 2.25%). NZD/USD at 0.5646. Watch for RBNZ's next meeting on 8 Jul for catalyst.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Carry + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.71, trading above the 160.00 level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings. The +263bp carry premium (SOFR 3.61% vs TONA 0.98%) continues to support the pair, despite JPY's positive CESI (+17). USD/JPY 25d RR at −1.47 indicates a moderate put skew, suggesting options market participants are hedging against JPY strength (USD weakness) despite the spot rally. This options positioning could exacerbate a sharp reversal if BoJ intervenes. Intervention risk remains elevated on sustained moves above 160.00.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Hawkishness vs USD Strength + Vol Premium
signals.text: ECB's Schnabel stated 'More hiking is needed to get to 2%', signaling continued hawkishness for the Eurozone. However, EUR/USD at 1.1361 faces strong USD tailwinds from speculation of tighter Fed policy and positive US data surprises (USD CESI +26). The pair's IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.9% (VRP +2.2%) indicates elevated options hedging demand, suggesting caution despite the ECB's hawkish rhetoric. The +143bp carry (SOFR 3.61% vs ESTR 2.18%) continues to favor USD. Watch for the next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 23 Jul for further policy clarity.

signals.title: AUD/USD — COT Long vs Negative CESI + Extreme Vol Premium
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +38,548 (long%=70.2%) indicates a significant speculative long position, which is at risk given the AUD's moderate negative CESI (-8 misses) and the pair's 1W performance of -1.55%. AUD/USD at 0.6904. The implied volatility (IV 20.6%) is significantly above realized volatility (HV30 8.5%), resulting in an extreme VRP of +12.1%. This elevated vol premium suggests strong hedging demand for AUD downside protection, contradicting the crowded long positioning. An unwind of these long positions is likely to accelerate if negative data persists. Watch for next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 11 Aug.
